# Koji

> Documents in. Structured data out.

Koji is a self-hosted, config-driven document processing platform. It turns unstructured documents (PDFs, Word, images, scans) into clean, structured JSON data using local models or any OpenAI-compatible API provider.

## Docs

- [Getting Started](https://docs.getkoji.dev/getting-started/): Install, start a cluster, process your first document
- [Schemas](https://docs.getkoji.dev/schema-guide/): Schema authoring guide — field types, hints, arrays, enums, custom signals
- [Configuration](https://docs.getkoji.dev/configuration/): Full koji.yaml reference — cluster, services, pipeline, models, webhooks
- [Architecture](https://docs.getkoji.dev/architecture/): How the pipeline works (map → route → extract → validate → reconcile)
- [CLI Reference](https://docs.getkoji.dev/cli/): Every command — init, start, process, extract, test, bench, logs, doctor
- [API Reference](https://docs.getkoji.dev/api-reference/): Every HTTP endpoint with request/response shapes

## Quick start

```bash
pip install koji-cli
koji init myproject --template invoice
cd myproject
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
koji start
koji extract samples/invoice_sample.md \
  --schema schemas/invoice.yaml \
  --model openai/gpt-4o-mini
```

## Commands

- `koji init [dir]` — scaffold a new project from a template
- `koji start` — start the cluster (pulls pre-built images by default)
- `koji stop` — stop the cluster
- `koji status` — show service health
- `koji process <path>` — full pipeline: parse + extract
- `koji extract <md>` — extract from already-parsed markdown (fast iteration)
- `koji test --schema <path>` — schema regression tests against fixture files
- `koji bench --corpus <path>` — benchmark accuracy against a validation corpus
- `koji logs [service]` — stream service logs
- `koji doctor` — environment health check
- `koji version` — show version

## Key concepts

- **Schema** — YAML file defining what to extract: fields, types, descriptions, hints. Domain knowledge lives here, not in the engine.
- **Pipeline** — `parse` (document → markdown) and `extract` (markdown → structured JSON) steps defined in `koji.yaml`.
- **Map → Route → Extract → Validate → Reconcile** — the five-phase intelligent extraction pipeline. Parses documents into chunks, routes schema fields to relevant chunks via hints, makes minimum LLM calls, validates types, reconciles results.
- **Hints** — `look_in`, `patterns`, `signals`, `max_chunks`. Tell the router where to find each field.
- **Custom signals** — schema-defined regex patterns (e.g., `has_policy_numbers`) that the chunker uses for routing.
- **Categories** — schema-defined section labels (`header`, `line_items`, `totals`, etc.) that `look_in` hints reference.
- **Model providers** — any OpenAI-compatible API or local models via ollama. Format: `provider/model-name` (e.g., `openai/gpt-4o-mini`, `ollama/llama3.2`).
